method_atom: Analysis of a chain of a few coupled point-like elements whose mutual separations are driven through a cyclic, non-reciprocal sequence of deformations, producing net directed translation per cycle in the overdamped (inertia-negligible, dissipation-dominated) regime; closed-form expressions are derived for the per-cycle displacement as a geometric functional of the deformation loop, the linear force-velocity response, the dissipated power, and the conversion efficiency, together with the effect of stochastic fluctuations and phase coherence in the periodic drive.
object_atom: A microscopic self-propelling swimmer built from three spheres joined by two extensible arms, translating through a viscous fluid at low Reynolds number.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the overdamped cyclic non-reciprocal deformation-to-translation mechanism with its displacement, force-response, and efficiency accounting; the three-spheres-and-arms body moving in a viscous fluid is the domain-specific object.
